header.title: Ethereum Protocol Visualizer
header.subtitle: Watch how transactions flow through the Ethereum network - from your wallet to blockchain finality
header.hint: Drag panels between columns · Arrow keys to move · Drag bottom edge to resize

idle.title: Ready to Start
idle.description: Click "Create Transaction" or enable Full Auto to begin the simulation.

creating-tx.title: User Creates Transaction
creating-tx.description: A user (you) uses a wallet application (MetaMask, Ledger, etc.) to create a transaction. The wallet software constructs the transaction data: recipient address, ETH amount, gas limit, and gas price.

signing-tx.title: Wallet Signs Transaction
signing-tx.description: The wallet uses YOUR private key to cryptographically sign the transaction. This signature proves you authorized this transfer without revealing your private key. The signature is created locally on your device.

broadcasting.title: Wallet Broadcasts to Nodes
broadcasting.description: Your wallet connects to an Ethereum node (via RPC endpoint like Infura, Alchemy, or your own node) and sends the signed transaction. The node validates it and gossips it to peer nodes across the P2P network.

mempool.title: Transaction Enters Mempool
mempool.description: Each node maintains its own mempool (memory pool) - a waiting area for unconfirmed transactions. Your transaction now exists in the mempool of many nodes, waiting for a validator to include it in a block.

selecting-validator.title: Protocol Selects Proposer
selecting-validator.description: The Ethereum protocol (RANDAO) randomly selects one validator to propose the next block. Selection probability is weighted by stake (32+ ETH). This happens every 12 seconds (one slot).

building-block.title: Validator Builds Block
building-block.description: The selected validator's node pulls transactions from its mempool, orders them (often by gas price), and assembles them into a block. The validator runs an execution client to process state changes.

proposing-block.title: Validator Proposes Block
proposing-block.description: The proposer broadcasts the new block to all other validators in the network. The block includes the transaction list, state root, and proposer signature.

attesting.title: Committee Validates & Attests
attesting.description: A committee of validators (randomly selected for this slot) independently verify the block. Each attesting validator checks transaction validity and signs an attestation vote. Attestations are aggregated.

finalizing.title: Block Achieves Finality
finalizing.description: When 2/3+ of total staked ETH attests to a block across two epochs (~13 min), it becomes "finalized" - cryptoeconomically irreversible. Reversing would require burning billions in staked ETH.

complete.title: Transaction Confirmed
complete.description: The transaction is now permanently part of the canonical Ethereum blockchain. The state change (ETH transfer) is finalized, and the recipient can spend the funds.

transaction.title: Transaction
transaction.instruction: Click "Create Transaction" to simulate a user creating a transaction in their wallet, or enable "Full Auto" for continuous simulation.
transaction.walletOpens: {wallet} opens their wallet app and enters transaction details
transaction.walletSignsDesc: The wallet software uses {wallet}'s private key to create a cryptographic signature. This happens locally on the user's device.
transaction.walletSendsDesc: The wallet connects to an Ethereum node (Infura, Alchemy, or personal node) via JSON-RPC and submits the signed transaction.
transaction.propagatesDesc: Each node validates and adds the transaction to its local mempool, then gossips it to peer nodes across the P2P network.

participants.title: Network Participants
participants.subtitle: Who is involved at each step
participants.nodesDescription: Each node maintains its own mempool. Transactions propagate across thousands of nodes globally.

validators.title: Validators
validators.validatorsTooltip: Validators are nodes that have staked 32+ ETH to participate in consensus. They propose new blocks and attest to blocks proposed by others. Malicious behavior results in stake being slashed (destroyed).
validators.randaoDesc: Protocol randomly selects a validator to propose the next block. Selection probability is weighted by stake amount.
validators.attestationDesc: Each validator independently verifies the block and signs an attestation vote. This happens every 12 seconds (1 slot).
validators.consensusDesc: 2/3+ of staked ETH has attested. Block becomes finalized and cryptoeconomically irreversible.

mempool.title: Mempool
mempool.mempoolTooltip: The mempool is NOT a single location. Each Ethereum node maintains its own mempool - a local queue of pending transactions waiting to be included in a block. Validators select transactions from their mempool when building blocks.
mempool.waitDescription: Transactions wait here until a validator includes them

participants.title: Who Participates in the Ethereum Network?

user.name: User
user.description: You! Initiates transactions using wallet software

wallet.name: Wallet
wallet.description: Software that creates & signs transactions with your keys

node.name: Node
node.description: Computers that validate, store, and relay blockchain data

mempool.name: Mempool
mempool.description: Each node's local queue of pending transactions

validator.name: Validator
validator.description: Staked nodes that propose & attest to new blocks

pos.title: Proof of Stake (PoS)
pos.description: Validators stake 32+ ETH as collateral. They're randomly selected to propose blocks and attest to others' blocks. Dishonest behavior results in slashing (losing staked ETH).

finality.title: Finality
finality.description: When 2/3+ of validators attest to a block, it becomes "justified." After two such epochs (~13 min), blocks become "finalized" - cryptoeconomically irreversible.

lifecycle.title: Transaction Lifecycle
lifecycle.description: Created in wallet, signed locally, broadcast to nodes, sits in mempool, validator includes in block, committee attests, block finalized.

simulator.footer: Educational simulation demonstrating Ethereum protocol concepts. Not connected to actual blockchain.

footer.attribution: Educational simulation. Not connected to actual blockchain.

visual.title: Visual Learning
visual.description: See concepts in action with interactive diagrams

handsOn.title: Hands-on Practice
handsOn.description: Control the simulation step by step
